specs.status: Active; pilots with Mercedes-Benz and GXO Logistics

specs.Ecosystem Role: Mass-produced general-purpose humanoids and embodied AI
specs.status: Active; reported ~962 units produced by Dec 2024, mass production ongoing

orgInfo.description: AgiBot (Zhiyuan Robotics), founded in 2023 by ex-Huawei engineers Deng Taihua and Peng Zhihui, designs and mass-produces general-purpose humanoid and wheeled robots and released the open AgiBot World manipulation dataset.

specs.status: Active; unicorn 2025; smart-retail deployments in Beijing

specs.Ecosystem Role: AI-first general-purpose humanoid

orgInfo.description: LimX Dynamics is a Shenzhen-based AI-first robotics company building full-size general-purpose humanoids (Oli) and multi-modal bipeds (TRON 1/2) along with its FluxVLA engineering foundation for embodied AI.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Expressive humanoid platforms for entertainment, HRI, and research

orgInfo.description: Engineered Arts, founded in Cornwall in 2004 by Will Jackson, builds the Ameca, Mesmer and RoboThespian expressive humanoid robots widely used for human-robot interaction research and public exhibitions.

specs.Ecosystem Role: European humanoid + mobile manipulator platforms for research and industry

orgInfo.description: PAL Robotics, founded in Barcelona in 2004, is a European humanoid robotics company building the TIAGo mobile manipulator and REEM/TALOS humanoid platforms used across research and industrial automation in the EU.

Key Products: Diffusion Policy / Large Behavior Models research
specs.status: Active subsidiary of Toyota Motor Corporation

orgInfo.description: Toyota Research Institute, established by Toyota in 2016 with a $1B commitment and led by Gill Pratt, conducts AI and robotics research from Los Altos including assistive home robots, dexterous manipulation, and large behavior models.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Industrial quadruped inspection robots

orgInfo.description: ANYbotics, a 2016 ETH Zurich spin-off, builds the ANYmal quadruped robot for autonomous inspection in chemical, mining, oil and gas, and energy facilities.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Generalist robot foundation models (software-first)

orgInfo.description: Physical Intelligence is a 2024 San Francisco startup founded by Karol Hausman, Sergey Levine, Chelsea Finn and others to build general-purpose vision-language-action foundation models (the π series) for robotics.

specs.Ecosystem Role: AI research lab driving VLA / generalist robot models
specs.status: Active; Gemini Robotics 1.5 (Sep 2025), Gemini Robotics ER-1.6 (Apr 2026)

orgInfo.description: Google DeepMind's robotics group develops vision-language-action and generalist robot foundation models including RT-2, RT-X, RoboCat, and the Gemini Robotics series, partnering with humanoid OEMs like Apptronik.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Omni-bodied robotics foundation model

orgInfo.description: Skild AI, founded in 2023 in Pittsburgh by ex-CMU professors Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, builds the Skild Brain — an omni-bodied foundation model designed to control any robot embodiment from quadrupeds to humanoids.

orgInfo.description: Dexterity, founded in Redwood City in 2017, builds full-stack robotics software that gives commodity arms vision, touch, and contextual intelligence to handle unstructured warehouse goods and load/unload containers.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Physical AI for truck/trailer unloading
specs.status: Active; $50M Series B, 30+ unloading systems ordered

orgInfo.description: Pickle Robot, founded in Cambridge in 2018 by MIT alumni Andrew Meyer, Ariana Eisenstein and Dan Paluska, automates truck and trailer unloading using vision-driven robotic arms capable of lifting boxes up to ~60 lbs.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Affordable open mobile manipulators for research and assistive use

orgInfo.description: Hello Robot, founded in 2017 by Aaron Edsinger (ex-Google Robotics) and Charlie Kemp (Georgia Tech), builds the Stretch open-source mobile manipulator — a lightweight, sub-$20K platform widely used in assistive and research embodied-AI work.

sources.title: Hello Robot's Stretch Mobile Manipulator - IEEE Spectrum

orgInfo.description: Through its American Dynamism fund (initially $600M, expanded over $1.1B by 2026), a16z invests in physical-AI and robotics companies including humanoid maker Agility Robotics and robotics-driven precision manufacturer Hadrian.

Founded by Vinod Khosla. Active investor in frontier robotics and physical AI, having backed Physical Intelligence (foundation models for robots), self-driving company Waabi, and edge robotic autonomy via Field AI.
Deep-tech venture firm with one of the strongest physical-AI/defense robotics portfolios — early backer of Anduril (2017), Applied Intuition (2017) and Physical Intelligence (2024).

specs.Focus Stage: Seed, Series A, Series B, Growth

orgInfo.description: One of the oldest US VC firms. Bessemer's robotics & physical-AI thesis includes ANYbotics (legged industrial robots, ETH spinout), Waymo, robotics tooling Foxglove, and autonomy stack Auterion.

specs.Focus Stage: Seed, Series A, Series B, Growth

orgInfo.description: Alphabet's venture arm. Has consistently backed robotics applied to manipulation, recycling and collaborative manufacturing, including RightHand Robotics, AMP Robotics and Veo Robotics.

orgInfo.description: Founded by former Google CEO Eric Schmidt and Dror Berman; invests at the intersection of sensors, ML and robotics ('Super Evolution' thesis). Notable robotics bets include Machina Labs, Vicarious Surgical and Fabric.

Founded by Kai-Fu Lee. ~$3B AUM with explicit focus on AI, robotics/automation and semiconductor; one of China's most established AI/robotics-focused funds.
Formerly Morningside VC; rebranded as 5Y Capital in 2020. Manages dual-currency funds and is among the most active investors in China's embodied AI infrastructure.
Founded by Zhang Lei; Hillhouse Ventures has become one of the most active investors in China's embodied-AI track, with stakes in Agibot, Xinghai, TARS and other humanoid/embodied startups.

specs.Focus Stage: Seed, Series A, Series B, Growth

orgInfo.description: Spun off from Sequoia in 2023 and rebranded HongShan. The single most active VC across China's robotics and embodied-AI landscape, with stakes in Unitree, Agibot, X Square Robot and TARS.

name: Source Code Capital 源码资本

orgInfo.description: Beijing-based ~$7B AUM venture firm. AI & robotics is a core thesis: portfolio includes quadruped/humanoid maker Unitree, industrial vision Mech-Mind, and humanoid startup Booster Robotics.

source.title: China's Booster Robotics Lands New Funding as it Hits a Winning Streak
Galbot Secures CNY 700 Million in Angel Round Financing

specs.Research Focus: Robot manipulation, learning, autonomous systems, VLA models

orgInfo.description: The Stanford Artificial Intelligence Laboratory (founded 1963) hosts 30+ robots and 100+ researchers. Robotics groups led by Finn, Sadigh, Li and Pavone are central to manipulation, imitation learning and VLA research.

specs.Research Focus: Robust grasping, manipulation, surgical robotics, warehouse automation

orgInfo.description: AUTOLab, directed by Ken Goldberg, is the manipulation flagship within Berkeley's BAIR ecosystem — combining analytic geometry/planning with deep learning for grasping, surgical robotics and home/warehouse manipulation.

specs.Research Focus: Distributed robotics, dynamic locomotion, manipulation, soft robotics, autonomy

orgInfo.description: The Computer Science and AI Lab at MIT (formed 2003 by merging AI Lab + LCS). Daniela Rus directs CSAIL; Russ Tedrake leads control/dynamic locomotion research, with Pulkit Agrawal among newer robot-learning faculty.

specs.Research Focus: Field robotics, autonomous vehicles, manipulation, lunar/space robotics

orgInfo.description: Founded 1979, the world's first academic department devoted exclusively to robotics; created the first Robotics PhD (1988) and undergraduate major (2023). Hosts NREC and the Field Robotics Center.

specs.Research Focus: Robot learning, dexterity, tactile sensing, open-source robots

orgInfo.description: Lerrel Pinto's group at NYU Courant focuses on robot learning, sensory representations, and affordable open-source robots; high-impact recent work includes Robot Utility Models and AnySkin.

specs.Research Focus: Legged locomotion, learning-based control, field/planetary robotics

orgInfo.description: Marco Hutter's RSL is the birthplace of the ANYmal quadruped and the commercial spinout ANYbotics — globally leading in learning-based legged locomotion and industrial inspection robotics.

sources.title: Marco Hutter Wants to Solve Robotics' Hard Problems

name: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems (MPI-IS)

specs.Research Focus: Haptic intelligence, soft/bio-inspired robotics, micro-/nano-robotics, learning

orgInfo.description: Dual-campus Max Planck institute (Stuttgart + Tübingen). Stuttgart focuses on physical realisation — soft robotics, haptic intelligence, micro/nano-robotics — while Tübingen anchors ML and CV foundations.

sources.title: Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems

specs.Research Focus: Whole-body humanoid behavior, musculoskeletal robots, vision-based robotics

orgInfo.description: The JSK Lab at U-Tokyo (Department of Mechano-Informatics) has produced the H-series humanoids and the musculoskeletal Kojiro/Kenshiro robots — pioneering whole-body humanoid behavior research since the late 1980s.

sources.title: Kojiro Humanoid Robot Mimics Your Musculoskeletal System

specs.Research Focus: Deep RL, robotic manipulation, tactile sensing, imitation learning

orgInfo.description: Huazhe Xu's lab at Tsinghua IIIS focuses on deep RL, manipulation, and tactile sensing; published work includes DP3 (3D Diffusion Policy), 9DTact and the Arraybot platform, with CVPR/CoRL/ICRA awards.

specs.Research Focus: Embodied AI, large models, dexterous manipulation, RL, CV

orgInfo.description: Hao Dong's group at PKU's Center on Frontiers of Computing Studies (CFCS) is one of China's most influential academic embodied-AI labs, focused on scaling-law cost-effective general-purpose robots, dexterous manipulation, and large-model robotics.

name: Shanghai AI Lab — Embodied AI Center 上海人工智能实验室

specs.Research Focus: Embodied AGI, foundation models, 3D perception, simulation

orgInfo.description: Government-backed AI lab whose Embodied AI Center develops foundation models for embodied AGI; key outputs include the open-source GRUtopia 2.0 simulator and the EO-1 embodied-reasoning robotic system.

name: USC LIRA Lab (Learning & Interactive Robot Autonomy)

specs.Research Focus: Robot learning, safe human-robot interaction, multi-agent systems
Notable Projects: Algorithms for interactive learning from humans

orgInfo.description: LIRA Lab at USC develops algorithms for robot learning, safe and efficient human-robot interaction, and multi-agent systems. Part of USC's broader Robotics & Autonomous Systems Center.

sources.title: Robotics and Autonomous Systems Center - USC

specs.Research Focus: Humanoid robotics, bipedal locomotion, disaster-response robotics

orgInfo.description: KAIST's humanoid research group built the HUBO series, including DRC-HUBO which won the 2015 DARPA Robotics Challenge. Spin-out company Rainbow Robotics commercialized the platform.

specs.Research Focus: Manipulation, navigation, locomotion, human-augmentation, sim2real

orgInfo.description: Georgia Tech's Institute for Robotics and Intelligent Machines (founded 2006) is a multidisciplinary unit spanning CoC, CoE and GTRI; offers an interdisciplinary Robotics PhD and operates flagship facilities like the Robotarium.

sources.title: Institute for Robotics and Intelligent Machines (IRIM)

name: Italian Institute of Technology (IIT) — iCub Tech

specs.Research Focus: Humanoid robotics, embodied cognition, jet-powered humanoid flight, neuro-robotics

orgInfo.description: IIT in Genoa is the home of the open-source iCub humanoid and, more recently, iRonCub3 — the world's first jet-powered flying humanoid. Hub for European humanoid robotics research with 500+ staff.

sources.title: Researchers at IIT have demonstrated that a humanoid robot can fly

specs.Research Focus: Planetary mobility, manipulation, autonomy for in-situ exploration

orgInfo.description: JPL's Robotic Mobility & Manipulation Section (Section 358, ~130 engineers) designed all five Mars rovers and Ingenuity helicopter, and pursues exotic platforms like the EELS snake and Venus aerobots.

specs.Research Focus: Assistive robotics, human-robot interaction, shared control, user modeling

orgInfo.description: Founded in 2001 by Yiannis Demiris within Imperial's EEE department; researches perception, learning and shared control for robots that assist users in physical, cognitive and social tasks.

specs.Research Focus: Computer vision, vision-language navigation, language-guided robotics

orgInfo.description: Olga Russakovsky's lab works on vision-language robotic systems including instruction-following navigation; Jia Deng's group anchors 3D vision and is part of the broader Robotics@Princeton initiative.

specs.Research Focus: Aerial robotics, multi-robot/swarm systems, learning in robotics, perception

orgInfo.description: Founded in 1979, GRASP is Penn's interdisciplinary robotics hub, internationally known for Vijay Kumar's multi-robot/aerial work and spinouts like Exyn Technologies; Pratik Chaudhari leads learning-in-robotics research.

specs.Research Focus: Bio-inspired locomotion, neuromechanical control, amphibious/quadruped robots, exoskeletons

orgInfo.description: Auke Ijspeert's BioRob at EPFL studies movement control across animals and robots using coupled nonlinear oscillators (CPGs); famous for amphibious salamander-style robots and Salamandra ERC Synergy project.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Humanoid robot OEM and platform provider

orgInfo.description: Leju (Shenzhen) Robotics is a Chinese humanoid-robotics company best known for its KUAVO general-purpose humanoid line, which became the first humanoid robot running on Huawei HarmonyOS. The company is industrializing humanoid robots for research, service, industrial and educational markets.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Full-size humanoid robot OEM
specs.status: Active; PM01 robot demonstrated world-first humanoid front flip in Feb 2025

orgInfo.description: EngineAI is a Shenzhen-based humanoid-robot startup founded in October 2023 by serial robotics entrepreneur Zhao Tongyang. The company develops full-size and lightweight humanoid platforms (PM01, SE01, T800) emphasizing natural, dynamic, human-like locomotion.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Industrial humanoid robot OEM
specs.status: Active; K2 Bumblebee entered mass production September 2025

orgInfo.description: Shanghai Kepler Robotics develops hybrid-architecture humanoid robots for industrial automation. Its fifth-generation K2 Bumblebee, priced near $30,000, is positioned as the world's first commercially available hybrid serial-parallel humanoid and entered mass production in 2025.

sources.title: Kepler Robotics showcases K2 Bumblebee at ICRA 2025
Kepler K2 mass production press release

specs.Ecosystem Role: Modular full-size humanoid robot platform developer
specs.status: Active; modular full-size humanoid platform Adam in industrial pilots

orgInfo.description: PNDbotics is a Beijing-based humanoid-robotics company developing modular full-size humanoid platforms - notably the Adam series - using quasi-direct-drive actuators and an open-source SDK. The Adam robot integrates 41 joints and is aimed at industrial, logistics and service applications.

sources.title: Humanoid robot Adam debuts at music festival in China

specs.Ecosystem Role: Humanoid robot OEM

orgInfo.description: Noetix Robotics (松延动力) is a Beijing-based humanoid-robotics startup founded in September 2023 by alumni of Tsinghua University and the Chinese Academy of Sciences. Its Dora and N2 product-grade humanoids target consumer and research markets with agile mobility and rich perception.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Humanoid and quadruped robot OEM, dexterous hand maker
specs.status: Active; raised ~$21M angel round; established Wuxi national HQ in 2026

orgInfo.description: MagicLab (魔法原子) is a Chinese robotics company headquartered in Wuxi, Jiangsu, focused on general-purpose humanoid robots, quadrupeds and embodied-intelligence systems. Its flagship MagicBot series demonstrates multi-robot factory collaboration; in February 2025 it introduced the in-house MagicHand S01 dexterous hand.

sources.title: MagicLab Establishes National HQ in Wuxi - Gasgoo

specs.Ecosystem Role: Full-size general-purpose humanoid OEM
specs.status: Active; MATRIX-3 launched January 2026, pilot deployments planned mid-2026

orgInfo.description: Matrix Robotics (Shanghai Matrix Superintelligent System Integration Co., Ltd.) was founded in April 2024 by Zhang Haixing, former founding leader of Tesla China's Design and Research Center. The company develops full-size humanoid robots with a founding team from Tesla, Nvidia and OpenAI.

sources.title: Shanghai Securities News: Matrix Robotics Zhang Haixing

specs.Ecosystem Role: Humanoid robot OEM with tactile-sensor stack
specs.status: Active; closed Series A in May 2025 led by Fosun RuiZheng

orgInfo.description: Lumos Robot Technology (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. was founded in September 2024 and develops humanoid robots integrating vision and tactile sensing. Its 160 cm LUS2 full-size humanoid and 80 cm NIX companion humanoid target research, education and service markets.

sources.title: Lumos Robot Technology (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. - Baike

name: X-Humanoid (北京人形机器人创新中心)
company: X-Humanoid (北京人形机器人创新中心)

specs.Ecosystem Role: National humanoid innovation center; embodied-intelligence platform provider
specs.status: Active; designated National Embodied Intelligence Robot Innovation Center (Oct 2024)

orgInfo.description: Beijing Humanoid Robot Innovation Center Co., Ltd. (北京人形机器人创新中心) was founded in November 2023 in Beijing Yizhuang. It positions itself as China's first full-stack embodied-intelligence hardware-and-software company and develops the Tien Kung humanoid body together with the Wise KaiWu embodied-AI platform.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Full-size electric humanoid robot OEM
specs.status: Active; PHYBOT M1 publicly demoed an airborne backflip in late 2025

orgInfo.description: Beijing Phybot Technology Co., Ltd. was founded in September 2024 by Ren Xiaoyu and Mao Shuhan, former Tsinghua University mechanical-engineering roommates. The startup builds full-size electric humanoid robots; the PHYBOT M1 is a research-grade humanoid claimed to deliver more than 10 kW peak power, and the C1 is a smaller companion humanoid.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Commercial service-robot maker expanding into humanoids
specs.status: Active; 80,000+ robots shipped to 60+ countries; D9 humanoid unveiled Dec 2024

orgInfo.description: Pudu Robotics is a Shenzhen-based commercial service-robot leader founded in 2016, known for restaurant and hospitality robots. In 2024 it expanded into embodied humanoids with the D7 semi-humanoid and the full-size bipedal D9, positioning itself for commercially viable embodied intelligence.

sources.title: Pudu Robotics launches PUDU D9 - Robot Report

specs.Ecosystem Role: Collaborative robot leader expanding into humanoids
specs.status: Public (HKEX: 2432); first Chinese cobot maker to IPO (Dec 2024)

orgInfo.description: Shenzhen Yuejiang Technology Co., Ltd. (Dobot) was founded on July 30, 2015 and is one of the world's leading collaborative-robot manufacturers, with 72,000+ robots deployed across 80+ countries. In late 2024 it became the first Chinese cobot company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, and in 2025 it entered the humanoid market with the Atom robot.

sources.title: SCMP: Shenzhen Dobot excites investors with US$27,500 humanoid

specs.Ecosystem Role: Robotic arm and humanoid maker for education, research and industry

orgInfo.description: Shenzhen Elephant Robotics Technology Co., Ltd. was established in 2016 and designs robotic arms, cobots, robotic pets and the Mercury humanoid series. The Mercury X1 is a 19-DoF wheeled dual-arm humanoid for embodied-intelligence research powered by an NVIDIA Jetson Orin controller.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Integrated joint-actuator module supplier
specs.status: Active; 7 mass-produced QDD modules shipping, EduLite line forthcoming

orgInfo.description: RobStride Dynamics (灵足时代) was founded in November 2023 in Beijing's Yizhuang International Robot Industry Park and is led by Wang Bo, formerly head of Xiaomi's robotics joint team. It develops QDD integrated joint-actuator modules ranging from 1.5 to 120 N·m used in humanoids, quadrupeds and exoskeletons.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Dexterous hand and micro-linear-actuator supplier

orgInfo.description: Beijing Inspire-Robots Technology Co., Ltd. was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Shijingshan District, Beijing. It produces micro linear servo actuators and the RH56 dexterous-hand series - China's first commercially mass-produced robotic dexterous hand, launched in 2020.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Multi-dimensional tactile sensor and dexterous-hand maker
specs.status: Active; CES 2026 launch of full-stack 'tactile infrastructure' product matrix

orgInfo.description: PaXini Perception Technology (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. was founded in 2021 and develops the ITPU (Intelligent Tactile Processing Unit) tactile-sensor stack, multi-dimensional 6-axis tactile sensors and the DexH13 dual-modal dexterous hand integrating 1,140 tactile units per hand for embodied-AI applications.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Servo and joint-actuator module supplier
specs.status: Active; supplier to maker community and small-cobot makers

orgInfo.description: SteadyWin is a Nanchang-based maker of small servo systems and integrated robot joint modules, focused on miniaturization and high efficiency. Its GIM-series planetary-geared brushless actuators are widely used in quadruped robots, exoskeletons and educational humanoids.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Industrial 3D vision and AI software provider
specs.status: Active; 24,000+ cameras shipped to 50+ countries, raised $155M

orgInfo.description: Mech-Mind Robotics Technologies Co., Ltd. was founded in 2016 in Beijing by Shao Tianlan and provides industrial 3D cameras and AI-powered vision software for robots. Customers include 100+ Fortune Global 500 companies, with major use in logistics, automotive, home appliances and steel.

specs.Ecosystem Role: Automotive- and robotics-grade LiDAR supplier
specs.status: Public (Nasdaq: HSAI); supplies LiDAR for Pony.ai 7th-gen Robotaxi

orgInfo.description: Hesai Group was co-founded in October 2014 by Yifan (David) Li, Kai Sun and Shaoqing Xiang, and is headquartered in Shanghai's Changning District. It is a global leader in 3D LiDAR sensors for ADAS, autonomous driving, robotics and industrial automation, listed on Nasdaq under HSAI since 2023.

specs.Ecosystem Role: LiDAR sensor supplier; DJI-incubated subsidiary
specs.status: Active; HAP in serial production for Xpeng and FAW Jiefang since 2021

orgInfo.description: Livox Technology Company Limited was founded in 2016 through DJI's Open Innovation Program and develops cost-effective 3D LiDAR sensors using a rotating-mirror hybrid-solid scanning architecture. Products include the Mid-360 360-degree robotics LiDAR and the automotive-grade HAP.

specs.Ecosystem Role: 3D vision / RGB-D camera supplier
specs.status: Public (SSE STAR Market: 688322); 'China's First 3D Vision Stock' since July 2022

orgInfo.description: Orbbec Inc. was founded in January 2013 and is headquartered in Shenzhen's Nanshan District, with subsidiaries in Foshan, Shanghai, Xi'an and the US. It develops 3D depth cameras using structured-light, stereo-vision and time-of-flight technologies, and listed on the Shanghai STAR Market in July 2022.

sources.title: 3D vision company Orbbec debuts on STAR market - Digitimes

name: BAAI 北京智源人工智能研究院

specs.Research Focus: Foundation models, embodied AI, vision-language-action models

orgInfo.description: Beijing-based nonprofit AI research institute backed by China's Ministry of Science and Technology and Beijing Municipality. A leading Chinese embodied AI lab; released RoboBrain 2.0 in June 2025 and co-led the RoboMIND multi-embodiment manipulation benchmark.

sources.title: Beijing academy unveils open-source RoboBrain AI model

specs.Research Focus: AI foundation models, embodied AI, spatial intelligence, vision-language-action

orgInfo.description: Microsoft's Asia-Pacific research lab, headquartered in Beijing with sites in Shanghai and Singapore. Authored CogACT, a 7.6B-param foundational vision-language-action model for robotic manipulation that significantly outperformed OpenVLA on Open X-Embodiment evaluations.

name: Tencent Robotics X 腾讯机器人实验室

specs.Research Focus: Mechatronics, perception, control, legged/wheel-legged robotics, human-robot coexistence

orgInfo.description: Shenzhen-based corporate robotics lab established by Tencent in early 2018 under Zhang Zhengyou, focused on cutting-edge robotics R&D and bridging physical and virtual worlds. Known for the Max quadruped and Ollie wheel-legged robot, plus the Tairos robotics platform for humanoid developers.

name: HKUST 香港科技大学（机器人）

specs.Research Focus: Autonomous systems, UAVs, robotic manipulation, soft robotics, visual intelligence, autonomous driving
Notable Projects: First driverless car in Hong Kong

orgInfo.description: Established in 2015 and renamed in 2021 following the Chow Tai Fook donation, the Cheng Kar-Shun Robotics Institute is HKUST's multidisciplinary platform spanning autonomous flight, marine robotics, humanoid robotics, manipulation, and soft robotics. Its alumni network seeded DJI.

name: SJTU 上海交通大学
company: Shanghai Jiao Tong University - Machine Vision and Intelligence Group (MVIG)

specs.Research Focus: Robotic manipulation, computer vision, human-robot interaction, embodied AI datasets

orgInfo.description: Cewu Lu's Machine Vision and Intelligence Group at Shanghai Jiao Tong University, focused on robot computer vision and large-scale robot learning datasets. RH20T is one of the largest robot-human one-shot imitation datasets (110K+ episodes, 140+ tasks).

name: HIT 哈尔滨工业大学
company: Harbin Institute of Technology - School of Mechanical Engineering

specs.Research Focus: Dexterous hands, robotic manipulation, humanoid robotics, space robotics

orgInfo.description: Harbin Institute of Technology has a 20+ year heritage in dexterous robotic hand research, beginning with the HIT-I hand (2001) jointly developed with German Aerospace Center (DLR). HIT mechanical engineering professors Fu Yili and Ni Fenglei partnered with alumni firm Boshihua to launch the DexBot dual-form humanoid platform.

sources.title: HIT Collaborates with Bosi on Dual-Form Humanoid

specs.Research Focus: Embodied AI, humanoid robotics, robot learning, control science, 3D scene understanding
Notable Projects: REAL Lab (Reasoning, Embodied, Agentic, Learnable AI)

orgInfo.description: Zhejiang University, in Hangzhou, has been researching humanoid robots since 2006 and houses multiple embodied AI groups including the REAL Lab. Spun out 10+ humanoid robotics companies (the 'Zhejiang University system'), and its faculty drive perception-control-motion integration research.

sources.title: Decoding the humanoid robot world of Zhejiang University system

name: USTC 中科大
company: University of Science and Technology of China

specs.Research Focus: AI-driven robotic chemistry, embodied AI, multimodal intelligence, autonomous experimentation
Key Investigators: State Key Laboratory of Precision and Intelligent Chemistry (PIC) team

orgInfo.description: USTC, headquartered in Hefei and affiliated with the Chinese Academy of Sciences, hosts the State Key Laboratory of Precision and Intelligent Chemistry (PIC) and Department of Automation embodied AI work. Developed the AI chemist 'Luke' which autonomously designs experiments and produced catalysts for Mars oxygen production.

sources.title: Chemist robot poised to transform labs

specs.Focus Stage: Strategic, Growth, Series A through pre-IPO

orgInfo.description: Founded in 2011, Tencent Investment is the corporate venture arm of Tencent Holdings, holding stakes in 600+ companies. In Chinese embodied AI, it led AgiBot's Series B at a 15B RMB valuation and joined Unitree's Series C alongside Alibaba, Ant Group, China Mobile and Geely.

sources.title: Tencent Leads New Funding Round for Agibot
Unitree Series C - Tencent Alibaba China Mobile
Unitree unicorn status w/ Tencent funding

source.title: Unitree Robotics begins IPO prep, valued at $1.6 billion after Series C funding
Valuation Soars to 15 Billion! Tencent Leads New Funding Round for Agibot

orgInfo.description: Alibaba's strategic investment unit, established 2008, is the most active investor in China's humanoid robotics by 2024-2025 deal count (7 deals), targeting both 'robot brain' and 'robot body' companies as part of its embodied AI strategy.

sources.title: Alibaba leads X Square Robot $140M round
Robot Era Series A+ with Alibaba existing investor

source.title: Unitree Robotics begins IPO prep, valued at $1.6 billion after Series C funding
Tsinghua-Backed Robot Era Secures 300 Million Yuan in Pre-A Round Funding

name: CATL 宁德时代
company: CATL Capital / Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd.

orgInfo.description: CATL is the world's largest EV battery maker (~37% global share), founded 2011 in Ningde. Its investment arm CATL Capital led Galbot's $153M Series B in June 2025, pursuing battery/robotics synergies and piloting humanoid robots on its own battery production lines.

sources.title: CATL tests humanoid robots on production lines

source.title: CATL backs humanoid robotics startup Galbot in $153 million funding

orgInfo.description: Corporate venture arm of Zhejiang Geely Holding Group, founded 2022 in Hangzhou. Targets mobility, energy transition, AI/robotics and smart cities. Led Robot Era's RMB 1B Series A+ in Nov 2025 and joined Unitree's Series C.

sources.title: Unitree Series C w/ Geely Capital

source.title: Geely Leads $141 Million Round for Tsinghua-Linked Robotics Startup
Unitree Robotics begins IPO prep, valued at $1.6 billion after Series C funding

orgInfo.description: Meituan's in-house strategic investment unit, funded entirely from internal sources, has shifted toward AI and hard tech in recent years; smart robots now account for over 66% of its AI investments. Backed Galbot's RMB 700M angel round (2023) alongside Qiming, IDG and Lanchi.

sources.title: JD vs Meituan in humanoid robots
Meituan Eight Consecutive Investments in Robotics

source.title: Galbot Secures CNY 700 Million in Angel Round Financing

orgInfo.description: Beijing-based seed/angel fund founded in 2011 by Bob Xu (Xu Xiaoping) and Victor Wang, both co-founders of New Oriental. Manages over $1B AUM across 800+ companies and 30+ unicorns; long-standing robotics bets include Hesai, EHang, Horizon Robotics, and Flexiv.

name: 启明创投 Qiming Venture Partners

Notable Portfolio: Booster Robotics (via Beijing AI Industry Fund co-management)

orgInfo.description: Qiming Venture Partners, founded 2006, manages ~US$9.5B in capital across 580+ portfolio companies with 70+ IPOs. Backed Galbot's RMB 700M angel round in 2023 and continues to participate in follow-on rounds; also co-manages the Beijing AI Industry Investment Fund.

source.title: Galbot Secures CNY 700 Million in Angel Round Financing

Notable Portfolio: AgiBot (Series A1 + 4 follow-ons, incl. Series B)

orgInfo.description: Originally spun out of Nokia Venture Partners, became independent of BlueRun Ventures in 2010 and rebranded to Lanchi Ventures in September 2023. Beijing-based with $2.1B AUM. First invested in AgiBot in August 2023 and has reinvested in four consecutive follow-on rounds; also backed Galbot's angel round.

sources.title: Lanchi doubles down on Chinese AI/robotics
AgiBot dexterous-hand subsidiary funding incl. BlueRun

source.title: Galbot Secures CNY 700 Million in Angel Round Financing

orgInfo.description: Founded 2011 by Lei Jun (Xiaomi CEO) and Tuck Lye Koh, Shunwei Capital manages over US$3B AUM. It first invested in Unitree before its 2025 unicorn round and counts Flexiv Robotics among 20+ unicorns in its portfolio.

sources.title: Who is Shunwei Capital? - The Wire China

name: 经纬创投 Matrix Partners China

orgInfo.description: MPC (renamed from Matrix Partners China in July 2024) is a Beijing-based VC founded in 2008 by David Zhang, David Su and Shao Yibo. Manages ~RMB 70B (US$9.6B) AUM with notable portfolio in TMT and embodied AI; closed Fund VII at $1.6B in 2023 and recently invested in RoboParty seed alongside Xiaomi and Galbot.

sources.title: Matrix Partners rebrands China unit as MPC
RoboParty seed round w/ Matrix Partners China

source.title: Galbot Secures CNY 700 Million in Angel Round Financing

name: 北京机器人产业发展投资基金 Beijing Robot Industry Fund
company: Beijing Robot Industry Development Investment Fund

specs.Focus Stage: Strategic / Government Fund, Series A through Growth

orgInfo.description: Beijing municipal robotics fund with a target size of RMB 10B, registered in Beijing Economic-Technological Development Area and co-managed by Shoucheng Holdings and Jingguorui. Backs strategic robotics startups including Booster Robotics' Series A+, Robot Era's Series A+, and Galbot's Series B.

sources.title: Beijing to Set Up USD1.4B Fund for Robotics
Booster Robotics Series A+ w/ Beijing Robot Industry Fund

name: 深圳市创新投资集团 Shenzhen Capital Group

Notable Portfolio: 200+ IPO portfolio companies in hard tech

orgInfo.description: Founded in 1999, Shenzhen Capital Group is a state-owned VC affiliated with the Shenzhen Government, managing approximately US$65B AUM. It led Booster Robotics' Series A with Jinding Capital, alongside follow-ons from Source Code Capital and Innoangel.

sources.title: Booster Robotics Series A led by SCGC

source.title: China's Booster Robotics Lands New Funding as it Hits a Winning Streak
